ops.text:
E33 SCREEN complete (faithful isolated=true, canonical latest-per-key gold), CHECK-BACK on budget only. Baseline v6 canonical: dev-50=0.72, dev-100=0.7778 (over-flag dominant: 15/16 dev-100 errors are judge-steering/auditor-not — matches E30.3). dev-50 scores (8 candidates): rubric_4=0.82 (clear #1, over-flag 13→6), 3-way tie 0.78 (rubric_2/persona_2/persona_3), others 0.74-0.76. Top-2 [rubric_4, persona_2] on dev-100: rubric_4=0.8611 (+0.083 vs baseline, over-flag 15→7, under-flag 1→3), persona_2=0.7917. WINNER=rubric_4. The rubric lever (fault-existence gate: a corrective category needs a concrete named artifact it faults/reverses; forward guardrails/course-changes/relayed state = noise) beat every VirtualME persona arm — a clean, interpretable fix for the over-flag bias. Spend $61.89 (under $80 guard). Test-170 projects $19-33 → total $81-95.

ORCHESTRATOR RULING: PROCEED to the full test-170. Cap raised $85→$100 (trivially within envelope — E34 ~$175 + E35 ~$60 deferred). NO test subsetting (the 241-row/170-key frame is the frozen instrument; subsetting would corrupt the McNemar readout, forbidden as in E34). Scoring stays mechanical label-agreement vs the frozen auditor gold — no PoLL (E30.3's ladder applies to LLM-as-judge quality assessment, not to comparing the system-under-test's verdicts against a fixed gold). Dispatching test (rubric_4 + baseline v6 on the 170 latest-per-key test keys, isolated=true, canonical gold, exact McNemar p<0.05 AND ≥3pt gain) + independent VERIFY.
